content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcel PnecrrcEs ConaurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 9581+2329
(916) 322-5ffi . Fax (916) 322-0886
October 27,2N4
Vanessa Spilios
Brown for Attorney General
409 13th Street, Suite 800
Oakland, CA946l2
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-04-214
Dear Ms. Spilios:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the Brownfor Attorney
General campaign for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Acf).r
QUESTION
May the Brown for Attorney General campaign invest in an auction rate preferred
account?
CONCLUSION
No. The auction rate preferred account does not appear to satisfr the requirements
of regulation 18524, which allows investrnent only in certificates of deposit, interest-
bearing savings accounts, money market accounts, or similar accounts-
FACTS
The Brownfor Attorney General campaign is interested in investing a portion of
its monies into an auction rate preferred account. After speaking with a representative at
Opparheimer & Company, you believe this to be a secure investnent instrument for the
campaign, but seek confirmation before taking further steps.
' Govemm"nt Code sectiors 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
e.E


File No. A-04-214
Page No. 2
You provided information on auction rate investments in the form of the Nuveen
MuniPreferred brochure produced by Nuveen Investments, L.L.C. and a description of
auction rate securities produced by Smith Bamey.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires that all conkibutions to a candidate be deposited into the
candidate's campaign bank account, and that all expenditures bemade fiom this account.
A candidate may transfer funds from the cafirpaigr bank account to certificates of
deposit, interest-bearing savings accounts, money market accomts, or similar accounts.
These funds must be redeposited in the campaign bank accomt before expenditure-
(Regulation 185240).)
The Commission has authorized the transfer of campaign funds to interest-bearing
savings accormts held in financial institutions. (Ayala Advice Letter, No. A-95-017.) The
Commission has advised that such investnemt accormts rnaybemaintained only at state
and national banks, state and federal savings and loan associations, state credit unions,
and federal credit unions located in the state- (BauerAdvice Lettcr, No. I-91-181.)
Auction rate securities are described in the materials you provided and in the
CDIAC brief as stocks backd bymunicipal bonds (Nuveen brochure).2 Neitherstocks
nor bonds fit within the exception to the bank account rules articulated in regulation
18524 and prior Commission advice. You do not indicate that the investnent account
would be held in an appropriate financial institution. Forthese reasons, an investnent in
auction rate securities is not a "similar accormf comparable to certificates of deposit,
interest-bearing savings accounts, or moneymarket accounts p€ilnissible rmder
regulation 18524.
If you have any other questions regarding this matte,r, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Hal
Political Reforrr Consultant
Techni cal Assistance Division
Enclosure
HD:LMjg
J\Uploads\04214.doc
2 Also, a description of auction rate securities issued by Douglas Skarr, "Auction Rate Securities,"
Califomia Debt and Investnent Advisory Commissio4 August 2004 (Issrrc Brief) describes these securities
as long-term, variable-rate bonds tied to short-term interest rates.
